title: Higher Oil Prices Lift Sinopec Profit 28% in First Quarter
summary: China’s top refiner posted a strong first quarter as higher crude prices lifted inventory values and stable domestic fuel sales helped to offset pressure from disrupted Middle East supply. title: China’s Data Center Boom Could Double Power Demand by 2030
summary: China is on course to nearly double its data center capacity within five years, with 28 gigawatts (GW) of new projects expected to come online by 2030, adding to the 32 GW already installed as of the end of last year, according to new analysis from Rystad Energy. title: UAE to leave Opec in blow to oil cartel
summary: Move underlines long-running frustrations with group over production quotas

title: After Record $19.50 Premium, Saudi Arabia Eyes Sharp Cut to June Asia Prices
summary: The world’s top crude exporter, Saudi Arabia, is expected to slash its official selling prices (OSPs) for crude loading for Asia in June from the record-highs for May as the premiums of the Middle Eastern benchmarks eased this month. title: UAE Quits OPEC and OPEC+ as Hormuz Crisis Drags On
summary: The United Arab Emirates is leaving OPEC and OPEC+, effective May 1, the country's state news agency WAM confirmed Tuesday, ending nearly six decades of membership and stripping the cartel of its third-largest producer in one of the most consequential exits in OPEC's history.
